title: 1GiB layer limit?
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Images pull down using docker but fail with podman  Layers are very large and fail consistently at 1Gib.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
'/usr/bin/podman'  'pull'  'XXX/YYY/ZZZ:cc1d905']
Trying to pull XXX/YYY/ZZZ:cc1d905...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 55521dc71959 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 0b0cbaa13104 skipped: already exists
Copying blob e8ddce32b711 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2d473b07cdd5 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 99fd0fec0f5a skipped: already exists
Copying blob 057f1a5f99f8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob f0e51667e647 [========================>-------------] 1015.4MiB / 1.5GiB
Copying blob 85b2badac0ac done
Copying blob d16368f59c32 done
  unexpected EOF
Error: Error writing blob: error storing blob to file "/var/tmp/storage016327387/1": error happened during read: unexpected EOF


**Describe the results you received:**

Many times it fails at exactly 1.0GiB

**Describe the results you expected:**

Layer's are pulled like using docker.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Consistently fails.  Use artifactory as repository.

**Output of `podman version`:**
$ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.14.10
Built:        Tue Dec  8 09:37:43 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64


```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Latest version: yes
trouble shooting: yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @marcpawl!

@mtrmac, have you seen that happening before?  I _suspect_ a transient network error.
Could you be running out of disk space?  I think we cause 2*Sizeofimage to be used?  Image space is being used in /var/tmp by default.

“error happened during read” means this is the registry client reporting, so not local storage.

For now I see no reason to doubt that “unexpected EOF” is exactly what happens, i.e. the registry (probably cleanly, not 100% sure about this part) closes a connection before sending all of the data. That could be a timeout, some kind of proxy (we’ve had reports of environments that let through connections with Docker’s User-Agent: but no other), or perhaps a server-side setting.
@marcpawl, can you elaborate a bit on the registry that you attempt to pull from?  What's your networking environment?  Is there a proxy in between?

@marcpawl could you please share the /tmp and /var/tmp entries of your /etc/fstab file? Because you mentioned that error comes at the 1GB limit, I think that /var/tmp is mounted to /tmp which maybe a 1GB size tmpfs filesystem which gets full.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have had no feedback in the past month, I am going to close,  If more feedback happens we will reopen.
Im facing the same problem here. when using docker pull it work find but podman will failed.
Can you provide additional details - what image, where are you pulling from, output of a `podman pull --log-level=debug` on the image?
